conditions: Post-accident orthopedic injuries (pelvic/hip fracture with surgical repair)
Chronic pain related to injury recovery
Insomnia secondary to pain and rehab schedule
medications: Cetirizine 10 mg as needed for allergies
Gabapentin at night for nerve pain (provider-directed)
health.mental_health: Feels frustrated and restless about reduced independence after the accident; has occasional anxiety about re-injury and is working on coping strategies in counseling recommended by her rehab team.
recent_visits: Outpatient physical therapy (3x/week) at Vanderbilt Orthopaedics Rehab
Orthopedic follow-up imaging and gait assessment (last month)
Neurology consult for post-concussion headaches (6 weeks ago)
Primary care visit for anemia labs and fatigue evaluation (2 months ago)

finance.income_range: $3,500–$6,000/month (mix of military retirement/disability-related benefits and part-time consulting)
finance.debt: Ongoing medical bills from the accident (deductibles, imaging, and therapy copays) and a small remaining balance on an auto loan; she is on a payment plan with the hospital billing office.
recent_transactions: Physical therapy copays and rehab equipment purchase (cane, resistance bands)
Prescription refills (iron supplement, allergy meds, pain management)
Ride-share expenses to appointments when she can’t drive
Monthly payment toward hospital payment plan

relationships: Marisol Morales (mother, frequent caregiver and transportation help)
Sgt. Kendra Lewis (former unit mentor, checks in weekly)
Evan Patel (close friend from Nashville community college, helps with errands)
PT group chat friends from rehab (support and accountability)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile online; posts occasional Nashville photos and a vague rehab milestone update but avoids details about injuries, benefits, or where she lives. Uses private Instagram stories for close friends only.
private_conversations: Discussing pain levels, mobility setbacks, and fears about long-term limitations
Talking about a potential medical board/disability rating review and what it means financially
Conflict with a family member about reliance on help and boundaries at home
Dating concerns and body-image insecurity after surgery scars

schedule.work_hours: 8–15 hours/week, mostly remote; typically two half-days of calls and one flexible research/writing block
regular_appointments: Monthly lab check or follow-up for anemia as ordered
upcoming_events: Orthopedic follow-up appointment and clearance discussion (next month)
Adaptive fitness intro session at the YMCA (next week)
Friend’s small birthday dinner in East Nashville (in two weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes up early due to soreness, does a short mobility routine, logs symptoms for PT, completes consulting tasks in short focused blocks, rests midday, and finishes with home exercises and meal prep to support recovery.

professional.company: Freelance consultant contracting with a small defense and emergency-management advisory firm in the Nashville area (e.g., a veteran-owned LLC supporting training and readiness documentation)
projects: Reviewing training SOPs and updating readiness checklists for a client
Creating a short briefing deck on risk management and incident response
Assisting with grant/report writing for a public-safety preparedness program
professional.performance_issues: Occasional missed or rescheduled calls due to PT, pain flare-ups, and post-concussion headaches; she has requested flexible deadlines and avoids long in-person meetings while recovering.
workplace_relationships: Works primarily with a project lead, Clay Henson, who is supportive but expects quick turnaround
Coordinates with a client point-of-contact who prefers phone calls, which can be hard on headache days
Maintains a veteran network for referrals but keeps her medical details private

hobbies: Listening to podcasts about military history and true crime
habits: Uses a cane on longer outings and avoids stairs when possible
Tracks pain, sleep, and mobility metrics in a notes app
Keeps blackout curtains and limits screen brightness to reduce headaches
Occasionally uses THC gummies for sleep/pain when symptoms spike (kept private due to stigma and work considerations)
lifestyle.living_situation: Staying with her mother in a two-bedroom apartment in the Nashville area during recovery; her room is set up to minimize fall risk, and she’s working toward moving back to her own place once she can drive reliably.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Focuses on iron-rich foods (beans, leafy greens, lean red meat occasionally) and vitamin C pairings; limits alcohol and caffeine because they worsen sleep and headaches; keeps allergy season foods simple when congestion is bad.
